title: toxicodendron trilobatum
intro: Toxicodendron trilobatum, also known as the Poison Ash or Poison Ash Plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and topical treatments, although it is essential to note that handling the plant can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions due to its urushiol content.
